description: Cross-specialist boundary-collision evals per guild-plan.md §15.2 risk #1 (cross-group trigger collisions) and §12 (specialist creation boundary gates). Each case targets a plausible collision axis between two specialists; the expected_specialist is the correct owner per their frontmatter description's TRIGGER / DO NOT TRIGGER clauses. Null expected_specialist means the prompt is too generic to route to any single specialist and should fall through to /guild:team-compose.

cases.prompt: Design the API for our new billing service — what endpoints, payloads, and error shapes should we expose?
cases.rationale: API contract (path/verb/payload/errors) is backend's lane; architect's 'design' is cross-component systems shape, not the REST surface.

cases.prompt: Design the billing subsystem: which services own it, how does it talk to accounts, and where does the event bus fit?
cases.rationale: Cross-component systems design with bounded contexts and integration seams is architect's lane, not the API contract backend owns.

cases.prompt: Write the POST /invoices endpoint with idempotency keys and the 422 envelope.
cases.rationale: Endpoint implementation with idempotency and error envelope is backend; architect does not write handler code.

cases.prompt: Write an ADR for why we're splitting payments into its own service.
cases.rationale: ADR authoring for architecturally significant decisions is architect; backend implements after the ADR.

cases.prompt: Deploy the checkout service to staging with a canary rollout.
cases.rationale: Deploy/canary/rollout are devops triggers; backend specifies runtime needs, devops wires the pipeline.

cases.prompt: The checkout service needs a new POST /refunds endpoint wired to the refund handler.
cases.rationale: Endpoint + handler wiring is backend's API/implementation lane, not a deploy task.

cases.prompt: Set up the Terraform for the new Postgres RDS instance and the VPC security group.
cases.rationale: IaC / Terraform is devops; backend consumes the DB but does not provision infrastructure.

cases.prompt: Write the migration to add a nullable 'deleted_at' column on orders with a backfill plan.
cases.rationale: Schema migration with expand-contract and backfill is backend; devops does not write DDL migrations.

cases.prompt: Add tests for the new POST /refunds endpoint so the contract is pinned.
cases.rationale: Pinning tests written alongside the endpoint (engineering-group TDD default) are backend's; qa owns suite strategy, not endpoint-level pinning tests the author writes TDD-first.

cases.prompt: We keep seeing flaky tests on the refunds suite — figure out why and quarantine them.
cases.rationale: Flaky-test investigation and quarantine is qa's explicit trigger; backend does not own suite-level flakiness work.

cases.prompt: We need a property-based test strategy for the invoice calculator.
cases.rationale: Property-based test strategy is qa's lane; backend writes pinning tests but qa owns property/fuzz/snapshot strategy.

cases.prompt: What coverage target should we enforce for the billing package?
cases.rationale: Coverage decisions and thresholds are qa; backend complies with the gate qa sets.

cases.prompt: Review this JWT auth endpoint for security issues.
cases.rationale: Auth-flow review and JWT review are explicit security triggers; backend writes auth code, security reviews it.

cases.prompt: Implement the JWT-verification middleware on the /admin routes.
cases.rationale: Writing the auth-enforcing code itself is backend; security reviews but does not implement.

cases.prompt: Run a threat model on the new password-reset flow.
cases.rationale: Threat modeling is a core security trigger; backend implements the flow, security produces the threat model.

cases.prompt: Audit our dependencies for known CVEs and produce an SBOM.
cases.rationale: CVE audit + SBOM are explicit security triggers; backend does not run dependency audits.

cases.prompt: Set up CI coverage gates for the monorepo.
cases.rationale: Picking the coverage gate (what threshold, which metric) is qa's call; devops wires whatever qa decides. This prompt is about choosing the gate.

cases.prompt: Wire the coverage check qa picked into the GitHub Actions pipeline so failing coverage blocks the merge.
cases.rationale: Wiring a named gate into CI is devops; qa already decided the gate, this is pipeline plumbing.

cases.prompt: We want to add fuzz tests to the release pipeline — what should we run and how often?
cases.rationale: Fuzz-test strategy (what to run, cadence) is qa; devops implements the pipeline step afterward.

cases.prompt: Add a blue/green deploy step to the production pipeline.
cases.rationale: Blue/green rollout is an explicit devops trigger; qa has no role in deploy strategy.

cases.prompt: The iOS app's API client is timing out intermittently — fix the retry logic in the Swift networking layer.
cases.rationale: Swift networking-layer retry logic is iOS client code — mobile's lane. Backend owns the server; the client implementation is mobile.

cases.prompt: The /sync endpoint is returning 504s under load — investigate the server timeout and fix the queue consumer.
cases.rationale: Server-side 504 + queue consumer is backend; the client's side is not implicated here.

cases.prompt: Improve cold-start time on Android — the app takes 4s to first frame.
cases.rationale: Cold-start is an explicit mobile trigger and is Android-platform-specific performance work.

cases.prompt: Design the REST contract the iOS app will call for offline sync.
cases.rationale: REST contract design is backend; mobile consumes the contract, backend owns it.

cases.prompt: Write the launch copy for our new AI code-review feature — tagline, hero, three feature blurbs.
cases.rationale: A launch with tagline + hero + feature blurbs is a launch plan / GTM deliverable — marketing commissions; copywriter drafts once marketing sets positioning.

cases.prompt: Draft the body copy for our new pricing landing page, keeping the existing voice guide.
cases.rationale: Landing-page body copy executed against an existing voice guide is copywriter's explicit lane.

cases.prompt: Nail down the positioning statement for our self-hosted tier before we write any copy.
cases.rationale: Positioning statement is marketing's core trigger; copywriter executes after positioning is set.

cases.prompt: Design an A/B test for three headline variants on the homepage hero.
cases.rationale: A/B copy variants program (design, hypothesis, measurement) is marketing; copywriter might draft the variants marketing specifies.

cases.prompt: Document the pricing page — explain what each tier includes and how billing works.
cases.rationale: Documenting what a tier includes and how billing works is functional user-facing documentation — technical-writer's user-manual lane.

cases.prompt: Rewrite the pricing page hero to be more persuasive — we want to convert more free users.
cases.rationale: Persuasive landing-page copy aimed at conversion is copywriter; technical-writer handles factual/functional docs, not persuasion.

cases.prompt: Write the migration guide for users upgrading from v2 to v3 of our SDK.
cases.rationale: Migration guide is an explicit technical-writer trigger (how-to / walkthrough).

cases.prompt: Draft the welcome email sequence for users who just upgraded to the Pro tier.
cases.rationale: Welcome / lifecycle email sequence is an explicit copywriter trigger; technical-writer does not own marketing emails.

cases.prompt: Write a follow-up email for prospects who took a demo last week but haven't replied.
cases.rationale: Post-meeting follow-up cadence to prospects is sales' follow-up-sequence trigger; copywriter handles lifecycle for existing users, not prospect follow-up.

cases.prompt: Write a lifecycle email nudging existing Pro users who haven't activated the new AI feature.
cases.rationale: Lifecycle email to existing users is copywriter's explicit lane; sales owns cold prospecting and post-meeting follow-up.

cases.prompt: Draft a cold outreach sequence targeting VPs of Engineering at 500-to-2000-person SaaS companies.
cases.rationale: Cold outreach + prospecting sequence is sales' core trigger.

cases.prompt: Write the welcome series for brand-new trial signups — five emails across two weeks.
cases.rationale: Welcome series / lifecycle email sequence is an explicit copywriter trigger.

cases.prompt: Write an X thread announcing our new AI code-review feature, 8 tweets, punchy.
cases.rationale: X / Twitter thread is an explicit social-media trigger; copywriter does long-form, social-media does platform-native.

cases.prompt: Write a 1500-word blog post about why we built the AI code-review feature.
cases.rationale: Long-form blog post is copywriter's explicit lane; social-media handles short-form platform-native content.

cases.prompt: Build the content calendar for next month across LinkedIn and X.
cases.rationale: Content calendar / posting schedule is an explicit social-media trigger.

cases.prompt: Repackage our latest blog post as a LinkedIn carousel with 10 slides.
cases.rationale: Carousel + repackaging blog-as-social is explicitly listed in social-media's triggers.

cases.prompt: Optimize our pricing landing page — improve the meta title, description, and on-page structure.
cases.rationale: Meta title/description + on-page SEO are explicit seo triggers; marketing owns positioning, not meta-tag work.

cases.prompt: Rework how we position our pricing page against Competitor X — their free tier is stealing signups.
cases.rationale: Competitive positioning is marketing's core lane; SEO would not touch messaging strategy.

cases.prompt: Run keyword research around 'ai code review' and related intents for the next content push.
cases.rationale: Keyword research + search intent are explicit seo triggers.

cases.prompt: Write the launch plan for our new self-hosted tier including channels, timeline, and KPIs.
cases.rationale: GTM / launch plan is marketing's explicit trigger; SEO is a tactic, not the plan.

cases.prompt: Email this list of 200 prospects individually about our launch — cold outreach, ask for a demo.
cases.rationale: Cold outreach to a prospect list asking for a demo is sales' outbound motion; 'list of prospects' + 'ask for demo' = prospecting.

cases.prompt: Broadcast to our full newsletter audience announcing the new launch.
cases.rationale: Broadcast to existing audience about a launch is marketing's GTM lane, not outbound sales.

cases.prompt: Write a discovery script for qualifying mid-market prospects using MEDDIC.
cases.rationale: Discovery script + MEDDIC + qualification are explicit sales triggers.

cases.prompt: Put together the campaign brief for Q3's enterprise launch campaign.
cases.rationale: Campaign brief is an explicit marketing trigger.

cases.prompt: Survey the options for our event bus — Kafka vs NATS vs Redpanda — produce a comparison table.
cases.rationale: 'Survey the options' + comparison table = researcher's explicit lane; researcher supplies inputs, architect decides later.

cases.prompt: Pick between Kafka and NATS for our event bus and write an ADR for the decision.
cases.rationale: Scoring options against criteria to pick one plus ADR authoring is architect; researcher produces the comparison but does not decide.

cases.prompt: Digest the last three months of papers on vector-database indexing approaches.
cases.rationale: Paper digest is an explicit researcher trigger.

cases.prompt: Run a dependency audit and flag anything with a known CVE above medium.
cases.rationale: Dependency / CVE audit is security's explicit trigger; qa does not own vuln scanning.

cases.prompt: Build a regression suite that catches the race condition that broke prod last month.
cases.rationale: Regression-suite authoring is qa's lane; security does threat models, not suite construction.

cases.prompt: Scan the repo for leaked API keys and secrets that slipped into git history.
cases.rationale: Secrets scan / leaked credential is security's explicit trigger.

cases.prompt: Set up EAS build + TestFlight submission for the iOS app.
cases.rationale: EAS + TestFlight submission is explicitly mobile-owned (mobile owns EAS/Fastlane/Xcode Cloud per its description), not devops.

cases.prompt: Wire up the backend API's GitHub Actions deployment to the Kubernetes cluster.
cases.rationale: Backend CI/CD + Kubernetes is devops' lane; mobile owns mobile-specific build pipelines only.

cases.prompt: Configure Fastlane lanes for our Android release, including Play Console metadata upload.
cases.rationale: Fastlane + Play Console is explicitly mobile's build/release territory.

cases.prompt: Run the tests.
cases.rationale: Too generic — no specialist-specific trigger. Should fall through to /guild:team-compose or a tooling path, not route to any specialist.

cases.prompt: Help me with my project.
cases.rationale: No domain signal; this is a /guild brainstorm entry point, not a specialist trigger.

cases.prompt: What should we work on next?
cases.rationale: Open-ended planning question — belongs to /guild:team-compose / brainstorm, not any specialist.

cases.prompt: Fix the typo on line 14.
cases.rationale: Trivial edit — no specialist should own a one-line typo fix; main session handles it directly.
